title: phegopyrum esculentum
intro: Phegopyrum esculentum, also known as Korean millet or foxtail millet, is a type of small-grained cereal crop that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting effects. Phegopyrum esculentum is known to have various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving digestion, and aiding in weight management. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including tea, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
